jokes.text: And bigger boobs.
jokes.setup: Phoebe lists noble wishes for omnipotence: world peace, no hunger, environmental good
jokes.punchline: Ends with shallow personal desire for bigger boobs
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke with perfect economy. Sets up noble aspirations then pivots to vanity. Very Phoebe-specific delivery.

jokes.text: Well, see, you took mine.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's shallow wish after noble ones
jokes.punchline: Monica admits she also wanted bigger boobs
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that extends the joke naturally. Monica's competitive nature showing even about boobs.

jokes.text: If I were omnipotent for a day, I'd make myself omnipotent forever.
jokes.setup: The omnipotence hypothetical question
jokes.punchline: Logical loophole to maintain power permanently
jokes.explanation: Clever answer that perfectly fits Ross's analytical nature. Smart wordplay with good structure.

jokes.text: There's always one guy. "If I had a wish, I'd wish for three more wishes."
jokes.setup: Ross's clever omnipotence loophole
jokes.punchline: Chandler calls out the trope of wish-multiplying
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary that breaks the fourth wall slightly. Classic Chandler sarcasm about common tropes.

jokes.text: Probably kill myself.
jokes.setup: Joey asked what he'd do if omnipotent
jokes.punchline: Misunderstands omnipotent as impotent, says he'd kill himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay based on Joey's character ignorance. Dark but perfectly innocent delivery. Excellent misdirection.

jokes.text: If little Joey's dead, then I got no reason to live.
jokes.setup: Joey's misunderstanding of omnipotent
jokes.punchline: Explains he'd kill himself because his penis would be dead
jokes.explanation: Escalates the wordplay perfectly. Joey's genuine emotion about 'little Joey' sells the innocence. Peak character comedy.

jokes.text: Ross, I'm sorry. I had no idea. I thought it was a theoretical question.
jokes.setup: Joey realizes Ross is supposedly impotent
jokes.punchline: Apologizes sincerely for bringing up Ross's 'condition'
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that extends the wordplay. Joey's genuine concern makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Mom and Dad took us to the Grand Canyon?
jokes.setup: Monica mentions sleeping at Grand Canyon, Ross says they never went
jokes.punchline: Ross realizes he has no memory of family trip to Grand Canyon
jokes.explanation: Solid sibling dynamic joke. Ross's confusion is relatable family humor.

jokes.text: My grandmother has a new boyfriend... and they're both kind of insecure in bed, so... And deaf.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why she got no sleep
jokes.punchline: Elderly couple is loud during sex because they're insecure AND deaf
jokes.explanation: Good escalating reveal. The 'and deaf' addition makes the situation more absurd and explains the volume.

jokes.text: They reassure each other that they're having a good time.
jokes.setup: Insecure elderly couple having loud sex
jokes.punchline: They verbally reassure each other during sex
jokes.explanation: Continues the uncomfortable mental image. The specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Less than 100 steps from our place to here.
jokes.setup: Chandler has counted steps between locations
jokes.punchline: Joey points out he has way too much free time
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor showing Chandler's obsessive tendencies and Joey's blunt observations.

jokes.text: It's funny. My birthday was seven months ago.
jokes.setup: Friends wish Ross happy birthday and give him hockey tickets
jokes.punchline: Ross dryly notes his birthday was seven months ago
jokes.explanation: Ross's deadpan delivery of obvious deduction. Good setup for the real reason.

jokes.text: So you had an extra ticket... and couldn't decide who got to bring a date?
jokes.setup: Friends pretending it's his birthday
jokes.punchline: Ross deduces they just had an extra ticket and couldn't choose who brings a date
jokes.explanation: Ross's analytical mind cutting through their fake gesture. Exposes their selfishness humorously.

jokes.text: Well, aren't we Mr. The-Glass-Is-Half-Empty?
jokes.setup: Ross calling out their fake birthday gesture
jokes.punchline: Chandler's overly formal phrasing of glass-half-empty
jokes.explanation: Chandler's awkward formal phrasing makes the common expression sound ridiculous.

jokes.text: Eleven days before Halloween. All the good costumes are gone?
jokes.setup: Monica asks what's wrong with October 20th
jokes.punchline: Guesses it's about Halloween costume availability
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing misdirection. Monica's practical concerns versus Ross's emotional trauma.

jokes.text: Sex.
jokes.setup: Ross euphemistically describes 'consummating physical relationship'
jokes.punchline: Monica cuts through euphemism with blunt 'Sex'
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word deflates Ross's elaborate euphemism. Monica's directness vs Ross's verbosity.

jokes.text: Believe me, I told everyone.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if Monica told her sister about his sex life
jokes.punchline: Monica admits she told everyone about Ross's first time
jokes.explanation: Monica's oversharing tendency creating awkwardness for Ross. Sibling relationship humor.

jokes.text: The hell with hockey! Let's all do that!
jokes.setup: Ross says he'll go home and think about his ex-wife with her lesbian lover
jokes.punchline: Joey sarcastically suggests they all do that instead of hockey
jokes.explanation: Joey's sarcastic response to Ross's depressing plan. Good group dynamic moment.

jokes.text: What are you doing? / I have no idea.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to convince Ross to come to hockey
jokes.punchline: Chandler asks what Joey's doing, Joey admits he has no idea
jokes.explanation: Self-aware moment where character acknowledges their behavior doesn't make sense.

jokes.text: Look at the window! There's my name! Hi, me!
jokes.setup: Rachel excited about her first paycheck
jokes.punchline: Waves at her own name on the window like it's a person
jokes.explanation: Rachel's naive enthusiasm about basic employment milestones. Childlike behavior that fits her sheltered background.

jokes.text: There was a cave-in in a mine, and eight people were killed.
jokes.setup: Monica remembers getting her first paycheck
jokes.punchline: Associates it with a tragic mining accident
jokes.explanation: Darkly unexpected association. Subverts expectation of personal milestone memory.

jokes.text: No, I worked at a Dairy Queen. Why?
jokes.setup: Ross asks if she worked in a mine after her tragic mining story
jokes.punchline: She worked at Dairy Queen, no connection to mining accident
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist follow-up. Monica's confused 'Why?' sells the disconnect between unrelated events.

jokes.text: What's FICA? Why's he getting all my money?
jokes.setup: Rachel looking at her first paycheck deductions
jokes.punchline: Thinks FICA is a person stealing her money
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Rachel's sheltered background. Many viewers relate to paycheck confusion.

jokes.text: You can totally, totally live on this. / Oh, yeah. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Looking at Rachel's tiny paycheck
jokes.punchline: Chandler and Monica's unconvincing reassurance
jokes.explanation: Their obvious discomfort and false optimism about Rachel's financial situation.

jokes.text: I swear I've seen birds do this on Wild Kingdom.
jokes.setup: Rachel presumably doing some kind of physical movement/dance
jokes.punchline: Monica compares it to animal behavior on nature shows
jokes.explanation: Visual gag we can't see but Monica's comparison to nature documentary is amusing.

jokes.text: Look at you! You're so big! I can't believe it! / I know, I know! I'm a duplex.
jokes.setup: Old friend commenting on Rachel's size/appearance
jokes.punchline: Rachel describes herself as a duplex (housing term)
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay comparing body to real estate. Rachel's self-deprecating humor about weight gain.

jokes.text: And you should... really, really... It's time to stop, you look perfect.
jokes.setup: Rachel complimenting Kiki on weight loss
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly tries to stop complimenting when realizing Kiki looks too thin
jokes.explanation: Awkward social moment where Rachel realizes her compliment has gone too far.

jokes.text: Look, look! I have elbows!
jokes.setup: Kiki showing off her weight loss
jokes.punchline: Excitedly points out visible elbows from being so thin
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about extreme thinness. The enthusiasm for visible bones is unsettling but funny.

jokes.text: She never took them off. It's because we... Sorry, sorry.
jokes.setup: Ross reminiscing about Carol's boots during their first time
jokes.punchline: Almost shares intimate details then catches himself
jokes.explanation: Ross's tendency to overshare about his past relationship creating awkwardness.

jokes.text: Peach pit. / Yes, bunny? / Peach pit. That night, we had... / Peaches? / Actually, nectarines but... / Could've been a peach.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to remember details of his night with Carol
jokes.punchline: Obsessive focus on whether they ate peaches or nectarines
jokes.explanation: Ross's obsessive attention to irrelevant details shows his current mental state. Joey's confused participation.

jokes.text: Hey, that woman's got an ass like Carol's. / What? I thought we were trying to find stuff.
jokes.setup: Ross is dwelling on memories of Carol
jokes.punchline: Joey thinks they're supposed to find things that remind Ross of Carol
jokes.explanation: Joey's literal interpretation of helping Ross remember. His innocent crudeness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Sometimes Arturo lets me put the chocolate blobbies on the cookies.
jokes.setup: Rachel defending her job to judgmental friends
jokes.punchline: Proudly mentions putting 'chocolate blobbies' on cookies
jokes.explanation: Rachel's enthusiasm for menial tasks and made-up word 'blobbies' shows her trying to find dignity in humble work.

jokes.text: Well, your mom didn't tell us about the blobbies.
jokes.setup: Rachel's pride in chocolate blobbies responsibility
jokes.punchline: Friend's sarcastic response about blobbies not being mentioned
jokes.explanation: Good callback that deflates Rachel's pride with deadpan sarcasm.

jokes.text: And I brought Operation. But I lost the tweezers so we can't operate. But we can prep the guy.
jokes.setup: Monica planning slumber party activities
jokes.punchline: Can't play Operation without tweezers but can 'prep the guy'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay treating the board game like actual surgery. Monica's practical problem-solving.

jokes.text: That is the unusual activity. They wanna see if you're okay.
jokes.setup: Rachel hasn't used her credit card in weeks
jokes.punchline: Credit card company finds NOT using credit unusual and concerning
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about credit card company logic. Irony of fiscal responsibility being 'unusual.'

jokes.text: And I'm getting coffee. And it's not even for me!
jokes.setup: Rachel listing her life problems compared to friends' success
jokes.punchline: Getting coffee isn't even for herself, adding insult to injury
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the depth of Rachel's frustration. The detail makes it more pathetic.

jokes.text: Rachel has left the building. Can you call back?
jokes.setup: Rachel storms off during emotional breakdown
jokes.punchline: Monica's Elvis reference to explain Rachel's departure
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that fits the moment. Monica's way of handling awkward situation with humor.

jokes.text: No, Jack and the Beanstalk. / Ah, the other Jack.
jokes.setup: Monica confused about which Jack Phoebe means
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact 'the other Jack' as if there are only two
jokes.explanation: Monica's deadpan response to obvious clarification. Good economy with 'the other Jack.'

jokes.text: But, Pheebs, Jack gave up a cow. I gave up an orthodontist.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's Jack and the Beanstalk metaphor for Rachel's situation
jokes.punchline: Rachel points out she gave up a wealthy husband, not livestock
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Rachel's practical concerns versus fairy tale optimism. Cow vs orthodontist is solid comparison.

jokes.text: Oh, see, Jack did love the cow.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying she didn't love the orthodontist
jokes.punchline: Phoebe insists Jack loved his cow, defending the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - defending absurd fairy tale logic. Her commitment to the metaphor is funny.

jokes.text: Floopy? / Yeah. / We don't know where we're going. / It's all gonna come together and it's just gonna be un-floopy.
jokes.setup: Rachel describes her life as 'floopy'
jokes.punchline: Monica promises it will become 'un-floopy'
jokes.explanation: Creative made-up word 'floopy' that perfectly captures uncertain feeling. Monica's 'un-floopy' response shows friendship support.

jokes.text: Yeah, like that's a word.
jokes.setup: Monica using made-up word 'un-floopy'
jokes.punchline: Rachel calls out the non-word despite using 'floopy' herself
jokes.explanation: Rachel's ironic criticism of Monica's made-up word when she started the made-up word trend.

jokes.text: Well, because you just... I don't like this question.
jokes.setup: Monica asks what if Rachel's life doesn't come together
jokes.punchline: Phoebe admits she doesn't like the realistic question
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's avoidance of negativity and preference for optimistic fairy tale thinking.

jokes.text: I mean, what if all we've got are beans?
jokes.setup: Jack and the Beanstalk metaphor about magic beans
jokes.punchline: Rachel worries they might just have regular beans, not magic ones
jokes.explanation: Perfect completion of the metaphor showing Rachel's fear that her sacrifices won't pay off.

jokes.text: All you needed were guys hitting each other with sticks.
jokes.setup: Ross finally enjoying the hockey game
jokes.punchline: Joey reduces hockey to its most primitive description
jokes.explanation: Joey's simple but accurate description of hockey's appeal. Caveman logic that works.

jokes.text: Well, the label promises nutty goodness.
jokes.setup: Chandler calling to complain about candy bar
jokes.punchline: Formal complaint about unfulfilled 'nutty goodness' promise
jokes.explanation: Chandler's overly formal approach to trivial complaint. 'Nutty goodness' is a funny phrase to take seriously.

jokes.text: But you know that, or we'd be in the predicament room.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying it's an emergency at hospital
jokes.punchline: Calls the emergency room the 'predicament room'
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism for emergency room. Chandler's way of downplaying serious situations with wordplay.

jokes.text: My face is dented.
jokes.setup: Chandler got hit with hockey puck
jokes.punchline: Describes facial injury as being 'dented' like a car
jokes.explanation: Perfect word choice - 'dented' makes face injury sound mechanical. Great economy and Chandler's dry delivery.

jokes.text: I don't even have a 'pla.'
jokes.setup: Monica saying she doesn't have a plan
jokes.punchline: Phoebe says she doesn't even have the first part of the word 'plan'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay showing Phoebe is even less prepared than Monica. Creative way to express being completely lost.

jokes.text: Wait! Did you say G. Stephanopoulos? / Yeah, this one goes across the street. I gave him yours. Bonehead! Bonehead!
jokes.setup: Pizza delivery mix-up with George Stephanopoulos
jokes.punchline: Pizza guy hits himself while calling himself bonehead
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup for the main plot. Pizza guy's self-punishment is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: When you do that, you're only hitting yourself.
jokes.setup: Pizza guy hitting himself while calling himself bonehead
jokes.punchline: Monica points out the obvious logical flaw
jokes.explanation: Monica's practical observation about self-inflicted punishment. Deadpan delivery of obvious truth.

jokes.text: No, pretty much just a towel.
jokes.setup: Girls asking if George Stephanopoulos was wearing a nice suit
jokes.punchline: He was only wearing a towel when pizza was delivered
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup. Contrasts their fantasy of his formal appearance with reality.

jokes.text: Pheebs, who's George Snuffleupagus?
jokes.setup: Discussion about George Stephanopoulos
jokes.punchline: Rachel mixes up his name with Sesame Street character
jokes.explanation: Rachel's confusion shows her disconnect from political world. Mixing up serious politician with children's character.

jokes.text: That's Big Bird's friend.
jokes.setup: Rachel's Snuffleupagus reference
jokes.punchline: Phoebe matter-of-factly corrects about Sesame Street character
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's straight delivery of children's TV trivia continues the absurd tangent.

jokes.text: That's not for you, bitch!
jokes.setup: Watching woman take George Stephanopoulos's pizza
jokes.punchline: Sweet Phoebe suddenly aggressive protecting pizza they don't own
jokes.explanation: Perfect character subversion - gentle Phoebe suddenly vicious. The protective instinct over pizza they're spying on is absurd.

jokes.text: That guy with the toe thing, who's he sleeping with?
jokes.setup: Ross complaining about waiting at hospital while sicker people go first
jokes.punchline: Implies medical priority is based on sexual connections
jokes.explanation: Ross's paranoid theory about hospital corruption. Funny cynicism about healthcare system.

jokes.text: Now you're gonna be in a wheelchair the rest of your life, pally-wally....
jokes.setup: Joey pushes Chandler in wheelchair despite warnings
jokes.punchline: Joey's dramatic prediction of permanent wheelchair use
jokes.explanation: Joey's over-the-top consequence prediction for minor accident. Physical comedy with dark twist.

jokes.text: I am so very sorry. Can I sign you?
jokes.setup: Chandler in cast after wheelchair accident
jokes.punchline: Offers to let Joey sign his cast like it's an autograph
jokes.explanation: Chandler's way of forgiving Joey by making light of injury. Reverses typical cast-signing dynamic.

jokes.text: And then when you do, he's a preppy animal.
jokes.setup: Girls discussing George Stephanopoulos being shy
jokes.punchline: Monica imagines his hidden wild side using 'preppy animal' contradiction
jokes.explanation: Oxymoron of 'preppy animal' captures Monica's fantasy about reserved politician's secret passion.

jokes.text: I think Joey's like a little kiddie ride. You just have to put the quarter in and it just goes.
jokes.setup: Girls discussing the guys' bedroom personalities
jokes.punchline: Rachel compares Joey to a coin-operated kiddie ride
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that perfectly captures Joey's simple mechanical approach to sex. Highly quotable and specific.

jokes.text: Chandler's one of those repressed guys who you'd have to seduce.
jokes.setup: Continuing discussion about guys' bedroom styles
jokes.punchline: Phoebe identifies Chandler as sexually repressed needing seduction
jokes.explanation: Good character insight from Phoebe about Chandler's guarded nature requiring work to access.

jokes.text: I think it's an act. Chandler is more sophisticated than he seems.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying Chandler is repressed
jokes.punchline: Monica counters that his nervousness is fake sophistication
jokes.explanation: Monica's different take on Chandler's personality. Suggests depth beneath his surface humor.

jokes.text: I really don't want to.
jokes.setup: Friends asking Monica to picture Ross in bed
jokes.punchline: Monica's disgusted refusal to think about brother sexually
jokes.explanation: Monica's appropriate revulsion at sexualizing her brother. Simple but effective uncomfortable moment.

jokes.text: What is it, that she left you for another woman that likes women?
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his situation is complicated
jokes.punchline: Joey's redundant description of lesbian relationship
jokes.explanation: Joey's redundant phrasing shows his confusion about lesbian relationships. Character-specific awkwardness.

jokes.text: Louder. There's a man on the 12th floor in a coma who didn't hear you.
jokes.setup: Joey loudly announcing Ross's private business
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically suggests Joey wasn't loud enough
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm with good escalation. Coma patient detail adds absurd specificity.

jokes.text: It was my first time.
jokes.setup: Ross's emotional attachment to anniversary with Carol
jokes.punchline: Reveals Carol was his first and only sexual partner
jokes.explanation: Major character revelation that recontextualizes everything. Perfect timing and delivery of vulnerable information.

jokes.text: Oh, boy. Hockey was a big mistake.
jokes.setup: Learning Ross has only slept with one woman
jokes.punchline: Joey realizes bringing Ross to aggressive hockey game was wrong choice
jokes.explanation: Joey's belated realization about poor activity choice for Ross's emotional state.

jokes.text: Well, unless goose is a vegetable!
jokes.setup: Monica mentions vegetarian pate Rachel loved
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was actually made with goose meat
jokes.explanation: Monica's confession about lying to Rachel about food ingredients. Good twist on vegetarian expectations.

jokes.text: Now I don't feel so bad about sleeping with Jason Hurley.
jokes.setup: Monica's confession about lying
jokes.punchline: Rachel counters with confession about sleeping with Monica's ex
jokes.explanation: Rachel's escalating confession in response to Monica's lie. Tit-for-tat revelation structure.

jokes.text: Anyway, the valentine Tom left in your locker was really from me!
jokes.setup: Escalating confessions among friends
jokes.punchline: Phoebe reveals she was secret admirer in school
jokes.explanation: Phoebe joins escalating confessions with sweet romantic revelation from their past.

jokes.text: She was a big girl!
jokes.setup: Rachel questioning why Tom wouldn't send valentine
jokes.punchline: Monica's defensive justification about girl's size
jokes.explanation: Monica's shallow defense about why boy wouldn't like Rachel in school. Mean-spirited but in character.

jokes.text: Really? Well, at least big girls don't pee in their pants in seventh grade!
jokes.setup: Monica's insult about Rachel being big in school
jokes.punchline: Rachel counters with embarrassing Monica childhood memory
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in their childhood insult battle. Embarrassing specific detail makes it sting more.

jokes.text: I was laughing. You made me laugh!
jokes.setup: Rachel revealing Monica peed herself in seventh grade
jokes.punchline: Monica's defensive explanation that she was just laughing too hard
jokes.explanation: Monica's attempt to save face by blaming Rachel for making her laugh too hard. Defensive but sweet.

jokes.text: Man, can you believe he's only had sex with one woman?
jokes.setup: Back to discussing Ross after George Stephanopoulos sighting
jokes.punchline: Chandler still fixated on Ross's limited sexual experience
jokes.explanation: Chandler's continued amazement at Ross's situation. Good callback to earlier revelation.

jokes.text: You kidding? The guy's a freak. I judge him.
jokes.setup: Monica and Phoebe finding Ross's inexperience romantic
jokes.punchline: Joey harshly judges Ross as a freak for monogamy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey response - blunt judgment of what others find sweet. 'I judge him' is economical and funny.

jokes.text: I thought you were great in Silence of the Lambs.
jokes.setup: Chandler's facial injury from hockey puck
jokes.punchline: Compares his disfigured appearance to Hannibal Lecter's mask
jokes.explanation: Chandler's self-deprecating reference to his bandaged face. Pop culture reference that fits the visual.

jokes.text: I found it. Finders keepers, losers weepers.
jokes.setup: Ross asking kid for his hockey puck back
jokes.punchline: Kid uses playground rules to justify keeping puck
jokes.explanation: Kid using childish logic against adults. Sets up physical comedy with Ross having to deal with child.

jokes.text: I'm rubber, you're glue. Whatever you—
jokes.setup: Escalating conflict between Ross and kid
jokes.punchline: Kid starts another playground chant to deflect Ross
jokes.explanation: Continuation of kid's playground defense tactics. Shows Ross being reduced to child's level.

jokes.text: Now that was fun.
jokes.setup: Ross presumably gets his puck back through conflict
jokes.punchline: Ross's dry assessment of confronting a child as 'fun'
jokes.explanation: Ross's sarcastic satisfaction after finally having some conflict resolution. Contrast to his earlier depression.

jokes.text: We could've played Monopoly, but no!
jokes.setup: Monica stuck in uncomfortable Twister position
jokes.punchline: Regrets rejecting safer board game option
jokes.explanation: Monica's regret about game choice while physically uncomfortable. Sets up physical comedy ending.

jokes.text: I've got magic beans.
jokes.setup: Rachel realizes her life situation has improved
jokes.punchline: References earlier Jack and Beanstalk metaphor positively
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Rachel's growth and optimism. Completes the metaphor arc with positive resolution.
